title: sphaeralcea coulteri
intro: Sphaeralcea coulteri, also known as Coulter's globemallow, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the mallow family and characterized by its bright yellow flowers and green leaves. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its saponins, flavonoids, and alkaloids. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Sphaeralcea coulteri can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional medicine to alleviate various ailments.
